div.p: output_rows = (rows_A × rows_B) / max(distinct_A, distinct_B) = (1,000 × 10,000) / max(1,000, 500) = 10,000,000 / 1,000 = 10,000 rows

ul.li: Each order joins to exactly 1 user (FK constraint)
So output = number of orders = 10,000 rows
No row explosion because users.id is unique
Uses the larger distinct count as denominator
If A has 1000 unique keys, B's 500 values each match ~2 rows from A on average
